segments.title: HMS Victoria's forward guns, collision, and Jellicoe connection
segments.summary: HMS Victoria links an early all-forward heavy armament, the fatal 1893 Mediterranean Fleet collision, and John Jellicoe's later role in the Nelson-class design story.
segments.body: Victoria carried her heaviest guns forward, an arrangement presented as an early Royal Navy precedent for the later Nelson and Rodney. After grounding in 1892, she was lost the next year when Admiral George Tryon's converging-squadron order produced a collision; Tryon died, while John Jellicoe survived. The video closes by connecting Jellicoe's later Grand Fleet command and influence to the return of an all-forward battleship battery in the Nelson class.

evidence.note: The transcript describes Victoria's forward heavy guns, commissioning and grounding, Tryon's fatal convergence order, Jellicoe's survival, and the later Nelson and Rodney connection.
